questions.explanation: len() returns the number of elements in a sequence; the list has 3 elements.

options.D: An error because len() only works on strings

questions.question: In Python, what does the expression len([1, 2, 3]) return?

questions.explanation: Binary search halves the search space each step, leading to logarithmic time complexity.
questions.question: Which Big-O notation best describes binary search on a sorted array of size n?

questions.explanation: A histogram shows how values are distributed across bins for a continuous variable.
questions.question: Which plot is most appropriate to visualize the distribution of a single continuous variable (e.g., model inference latency)?

questions.explanation: A Bernoulli variable takes value 1 with probability p and 0 otherwise, so E[X] = 1·p + 0·(1-p) = p.
questions.question: In probability, what is the expected value (mean) of a Bernoulli random variable X ~ Bernoulli(p)?

questions.explanation: ROC-AUC evaluates ranking quality across all possible thresholds, unlike accuracy/F1/confusion matrix which depend on a chosen threshold.
questions.question: You have a Pandas DataFrame with columns: y_true (0/1) and y_score (probability). Which metric is threshold-independent and commonly used for binary classifiers?

questions.explanation: torch.no_grad() disables autograd tracking, reducing memory usage and ensuring no gradients are computed during inference.
questions.question: In PyTorch, which code snippet correctly prevents gradient tracking during model evaluation?

questions.explanation: A normalized design uses a one-to-many relationship: Experiments (1) → Metrics (many), enabling indexing and flexible querying by metric_name and step.

options.A: Store all metrics as a JSON blob in a single Experiments table row
options.B: Create an Experiments table and a Metrics table with a foreign key experiment_id in Metrics
options.C: Create a separate table for each metric name (e.g., AccuracyTable, LossTable)
options.D: Store metrics as a comma-separated string column in Experiments

questions.question: You are designing a relational database for ML experiments. Each experiment can have many metrics logged over time (step, metric_name, value). Which schema best supports this while avoiding duplication and enabling efficient queries?

questions.explanation: Exploding gradients can cause unstable updates and NaNs; gradient clipping bounds gradient magnitude and stabilizes training without changing the model objective.

options.B: Apply gradient clipping (e.g., clip norm) before optimizer.step()
options.C: Remove all nonlinearities to make the network linear
options.D: Switch the loss to mean squared error regardless of task

questions.question: You are training a deep network with SGD and observe the loss becomes NaN after a few steps. Which intervention most directly addresses exploding gradients while preserving the overall training setup?

questions.explanation: Reproducibility requires seeding all RNG sources used by the pipeline (Python/NumPy/PyTorch) and the environment/action space; logging does not enforce determinism.

options.A: Only set env.reset(seed=0) and ignore all other seeds
options.B: Set seeds for Python random, NumPy, and PyTorch; call env.reset(seed=seed); and use env.action_space.seed(seed)
options.C: Disable GPU usage; reproducibility is guaranteed automatically
options.D: Log metrics to W&B; logging alone ensures determinism

questions.question: You need reproducible RL training in Gymnasium across runs on the same machine. Which combination is most appropriate to reduce randomness from environment and learning code?

questions.explanation: Version control, reviews, automated testing, CI, and versioned releases provide auditability, validation, and reproducibility—critical for regulated statistical reporting.

options.A: Allow analysts to edit production code directly to speed up iteration
options.B: Use version control with code reviews, automated tests (unit/integration), and a CI pipeline that produces versioned releases
options.C: Rely on manual testing only, since automated tests are hard for statistics
options.D: Keep requirements informal to avoid slowing development

questions.question: Your team is building statistical software for model evaluation that will be used in regulated reporting. Which SDLC practice best ensures changes are traceable, validated, and reproducible over time?
